methodology: Cross-reference network analysis (including 34-vote connection — strongest in dataset) + patristic commentary reading (17 evidences: Chrysostom, Augustine, Aquinas, Bede, Ephrem, Haydock, Cyril, Theophylact) + CCEL major works (Calvin Hebrews commentary, Matthew Henry, Baxter Saints' Rest, Johnson PNT) + topic verification (REST, SABBATH, CANAAN) + theological progression mapping (6 levels of rest)

evidence: commentaries (Aquinas 'two senses of rest', Bede '7 ages', Ephrem 'true rest future', Haydock)
commentaries (Augustine 'joy and fear', Haydock 'David speaks of future rest')

relevance_change.reason: 34 votes to HEB.3.7 is the strongest cross-reference connection found in the entire dataset. This OT verse is the foundation of the entire Hebrews 3-4 argument about divine rest. Haydock confirms David spoke of a FUTURE rest, not Canaan.

evidence: commentaries (Chrysostom, Cyril, Augustine, Theophylact, Simeon — 6 entries)
ccel (Matthew Henry — 'begun in grace, perfected in glory')

new_refs_added.justification: 7 votes de GEN.2.2. 4 votes de HEB.4.10. Verso-ponte que cita Gênesis 2:2 diretamente dentro do argumento de Hebreus 4. Sem ele, a cadeia Gn 2→Hb 4 perde o elo citacional explícito.
34 votes de PSA.95.11 (citação direta — conexão mais forte do dataset). 6 votes de HEB.4.3. Hub da conexão Salmos 95→Hebreus 4. Texto: 'Assim jurei na minha ira: Não entrarão no meu descanso.'

5 votes de HEB.4.10. 4 votes de HEB.4.9 e HEB.4.11. Johnson comenta. Consumação escatológica do tema: 'descansem das suas fadigas, pois as suas obras os acompanham.' Completa a progressão teológica do nível 6 (descanso final).

candidates_noted.justification: 6 votes para MAT.11.28 e MAT.11.29. Eco OT do convite de Cristo: 'Este é o descanso, dai descanso ao cansado.' Paralelo com Sl 95:11 em 'não quiseram ouvir.'
3 votes de PSA.95.11. Linguagem quase idêntica a Mt 11:29: 'achareis descanso para a vossa alma.' Rejeição paralela: 'Não andaremos.'
3 votes de PSA.95.11. 13 votes para DEU.31.8. 'A minha presença irá contigo, e eu te darei descanso.' Descanso = presença divina.

theological_progression.description: 6 levels of 'rest' in Scripture, confirmed by patristic and Reformation commentators

levels.name: Descanso da Criação
levels.nature: Arquétipo — Deus cessa e se satisfaz

levels.name: Descanso de Cristo
levels.commentator: Chrysostom: 'total security'; Cyril: 'freedom from the Law'

theological_progression.significance_for_tcc: A hybrid search for 'descanso de Deus' should ideally retrieve verses from ALL 6 levels, showing the progression from archetype (Gn 2) to consummation (Ap 14). Pure lexical search finds 'descanso' in levels 1,3,4,5 but misses level 6 ('fadigas') and level 2 ('sábado'). Semantic search should connect all.

strongest_connection.note: This is the strongest cross-reference connection found in the entire bible-crossrefs-dataset (1.2M links). It represents a direct OT quotation in the NT.

framework: 3. Spiritual rest (Hb 4/Mt 11) — 'union with God', summum bonum

henry_formula.formula: begun in grace, perfected in glory
henry_formula.significance: Connects Mt 11:28 (present grace) → Hb 4:9 (future glory) in a single theological formula
